div1.entryFree: Beforehand, in anticipation: I see what crosses my attempt will bring, . . . all this b. counsel comprehends, Lucr. 494. O let us pay the time but needful woe, since it hath been b. with our griefs, John V, 7, 111, i. e. since the state of things has anticipated our griefs, has, forestalling them, engrossed all our care and attention. cf. Forehand.
